generalGuide.title: General Program Guide
generalGuide.subtitle: Guidelines to make the most of your experience in the Professional Exchange Program.
generalGuide.participantObjective: Guide the participant (mentee) on how to make the most of the exchange experience, detailing responsibilities, expectations, practical tips, and support resources.
generalGuide.roleDesc: You are the main actor of your development journey.

responsibilities.0: Be the protagonist of your own development.
responsibilities.2: Follow the proposed schedule and activities.
responsibilities.3: Maintain ethical conduct, respecting local norms and culture.
responsibilities.4: Record learnings and challenges in the Logbook.

beforeItems.0: Participate in the pre-exchange briefing with HR and manager.
beforeItems.1: Review norms, policies, and rules of the receiving unit.
beforeItems.2: Organize documents, tickets, accommodation, and insurance.
beforeItems.3: Sign data protection and confidentiality terms.
beforeItems.4: Study the receiving unit and align expectations with the mentor.

duringItems.1: Attend weekly meetings with the mentor.
duringItems.2: Fill in the Logbook with learnings, challenges, and suggestions.
duringItems.3: Communicate clearly and frequently with the mentor and origin manager.
duringItems.4: Request feedback and be open to improvement suggestions.
duringItems.5: Respect norms, policies, and culture of the receiving unit.

afterItems.0: Prepare the Final Report (A3), detailing activities, learnings, and suggestions.
afterItems.1: Present results to managers and team.
afterItems.2: Share best practices and lessons learned with colleagues.

tips.0: Be curious: ask questions, observe processes, and propose ideas.
tips.1: Build networking: interact with different areas and colleagues.
tips.2: Write everything down: record insights, questions, and learnings daily.
tips.3: Be flexible: adapt to new routines and cultures.

supportItems.0: Mentor designated by the receiving unit.

contactItems.0: Mentor name and contact (verified on the platform).
contactItems.1: Origin and receiving unit HR (available in HR Support).

generalGuide.managerObjective: Guide the lending manager on key precautions and best practices when releasing an employee to participate in the FREUDENBERG EXPERIENCE project, ensuring alignment, support, and continuity of team activities.

alignItems.0: Explain the objectives of the job rotation.
alignItems.1: Reinforce that it is an opportunity for development and visibility.
alignItems.2: Make sure they are comfortable with the temporary change.

communicateItems.0: Inform about the employee's temporary absence.
communicateItems.1: Reorganize responsibilities to cover urgent demands.

formalizeItems.0: Record the start and end date.
formalizeItems.1: Identify the responsible manager at the destination.

avoidItems.0: The employee will be 100% dedicated to the destination project.
avoidItems.1: Do not involve them in origin team activities.

facilitateItems.0: Be available for emotional support or questions.
facilitateItems.1: Encourage the employee to enjoy the experience.

integrationItems.0: Promote a return conversation with the employee.
integrationItems.1: Ask them to share learnings and perceptions.

reassessItems.0: Reintroduce the employee to activities with clarity.

generalGuide.shareFeedback: Share feedback with HR or leadership:

shareFeedbackItems.0: Evaluate the impact of the experience.
shareFeedbackItems.1: Suggest improvements for future job rotation actions.

generalGuide.reminderText: The Freudenberg Experience project is a reward offered to high-performance employees and should be used as such. Promoting the well-being and proper use of the employee ensures its continuity and positive impact on the participant and other employees directly or indirectly involved.
generalGuide.tutorialSubtitle: A quick guide to the main features and how they align with the program.
generalGuide.tutorialIntro: Welcome to the Digital Experience Platform (DPX)! This tool was designed to digitize and enhance your professional development journey. Use this guide to understand the main resources at your disposal.
generalGuide.dashboardsDesc: Your first contact with the platform. The dashboard view changes according to your profile (Participant, Manager, Mentor, HR), always showing the most relevant KPIs and information for your role in the program.
generalGuide.diaryDesc: This is your main tool for documenting the experience. It replaces manual forms and centralizes your progress.

diaryItems.0: Dynamic Recording: Create entries with text, attach photos of a whiteboard, or record a quick video summarizing your day.
diaryItems.1: Continuous Feedback: Mentors and managers can comment directly on your entries, providing real-time guidance.
diaryItems.2: AI for Insights: Use the "Generate Summary" button for the AI to analyze your notes, identify overall sentiment, and extract key learnings.

generalGuide.innovationTitle: Innovation Labs: From Idea to Impact
generalGuide.innovationDesc: A space to transform your learnings into real innovation.

innovationItems.0: Challenges: Keep an eye on strategic challenges launched by areas.
innovationItems.1: Idea Submission: Turn an insight from your Diary 4.0 into an improvement proposal. AI can help refine your title and estimate impact.
innovationItems.2: Sprints and Validation: The most promising ideas are selected for "Experimentation Sprints", quick cycles to test hypotheses and measure results.
innovationItems.3: Best Practices Catalog: Validated ideas become "playbooks" that can be replicated by other teams, scaling success.

generalGuide.learningDesc: Your portal for continuous development and knowledge sharing.

learningItems.0: Courses and Paths: Access content created by internal experts and organized into learning paths.
learningItems.1: Create with AI: Have knowledge to share? Use the "Create Content" tool for AI to structure a complete course for you, with modules, content, and even a quiz.

generalGuide.mobilityDesc: Resources to manage your career and connections.

mobilityItems.0: Exchange Center: Explore and apply for job rotation opportunities.
mobilityItems.1: Mentorship Center: If you are a mentor, use this area to closely track the progress and tasks of your mentees.
mobilityItems.2: Alumni Network: Stay in touch with former participants and continue exchanging experiences even after the program ends.
